cases.judge: The shared extraction and import redirection were implemented, but `send-comment` still retains local `ActionButton` in `send-button.tsx`, so scope is not fully cleaned.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: The patch adds `src/shared/ui/action-button.tsx` and `src/shared/ui/index.ts` exporting `ActionButton`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent create a proper shared/ui slice with the extracted ActionButton (action-button.tsx and an index.ts public API)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: There is no change to `features/send-comment/ui/send-button.tsx`, so any existing `ActionButton` definition/re-export there remains.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent eliminate the now-dead ActionButton definition inside features/send-comment instead of leaving it as a re-export shim?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `comment-form.tsx` now imports `ActionButton` from `@/shared/ui`; `like-button.tsx` in `like-post` was also updated away from cross-feature import.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent update all in-feature consumers (notably comment-form.tsx) to import ActionButton from the new shared location rather than from a stale local path?

cases.judge: The patch correctly routes `normalizeProduct` through the entity public API and minimally extends that API with a named export while avoiding wildcard re-exports.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: index.ts now has explicit named exports for `Product`, `ProductCard`, and `normalizeProduct`; no `export *` or additional re-exports were added.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does the updated entities/product/index.ts export only the symbols actually consumed externally (Product type, ProductCard, normalizeProduct), without leaking unrelated internals or using export *?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Widget import was changed from `@/entities/product/model/product` to `@/entities/product` for both `normalizeProduct` and `ProductCard`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does the widget import normalizeProduct from @/entities/product (the slice root) rather than any internal path like @/entities/product/model/...?

cases.judge: The patch correctly introduces a public API for `features/user-settings` and migrates the two listed external page consumers to it, with only a minor scope issue from adding an unrelated `AGENTS.md` file.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Both `src/pages/profile/ui/profile-page.tsx` and `src/pages/settings/ui/settings-page.tsx` were switched to import `SettingsForm` from `@/features/user-settings`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent update every page that imported from @/features/user-settings/ui/settings-form (both pages/settings and pages/profile), not only the one mentioned in the task?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: New `src/features/user-settings/index.ts` uses explicit named exports for `SettingsForm` and `useUserSettings`; no `export *` is used.
judge_binary_evals.question: Is the new features/user-settings/index.ts a minimal, explicit public API (named exports of the intended surface) rather than a blanket export *?

cases.judge: The patch satisfies the required FSD segment placement and minimal public API; all requested pieces are implemented and wired into the auth page.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Async login call and loading/error state are implemented in `src/features/login/model/use-login.ts`; `LoginForm` only renders fields and calls `login`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Is async/mutation logic (fetch call, loading state, error handling) in model/ rather than directly in the UI component?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/features/login/api/login.ts` contains `loginRequest` using `httpPost`, while model imports and calls it.
judge_binary_evals.question: Is the raw HTTP request function isolated in api/ and not mixed into model/ or ui/?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/features/login/index.ts` exports only `LoginForm` and `useLogin`; no internal helper exports (`loginRequest`, payload/response types) are re-exported.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does index.ts export only the public contract (LoginForm, useLogin) without leaking internal functions?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Feature files import only local `../model` or shared (`httpPost` via shared API); page imports `LoginForm` from the feature.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does the feature avoid importing from pages/ or widgets/?

judge_binary_evals.question: Does the updated entities/product/index.ts export only the symbols actually consumed externally (Product type, ProductCard, normalizeProduct), without leaking unrelated internals or using export *?

cases.judge: The patch satisfies the migration intent by introducing a minimal feature public API and rewiring both consumer pages to import through it.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Both modified page imports were updated to '@/features/user-settings': pages/profile/ui/profile-page.tsx and pages/settings/ui/settings-page.tsx.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent update every page that imported from @/features/user-settings/ui/settings-form (both pages/settings and pages/profile), not only the one mentioned in the task?

judge_binary_evals.question: Is the new features/user-settings/index.ts a minimal, explicit public API (named exports of the intended surface) rather than a blanket export *?

cases.judge: The patch is structurally compliant with the requested FSD slice layout, with clean layering, isolated API logic, and a minimal feature public API.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: The UI delegates submission to `useLogin().execute`, while `use-login.ts` owns `isLoading`, `error`, and try/catch handling around the request.
judge_binary_evals.question: Is async/mutation logic (fetch call, loading state, error handling) in model/ rather than directly in the UI component?

judge_binary_evals.question: Is the raw HTTP request function isolated in api/ and not mixed into model/ or ui/?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/features/login/index.ts` exports only `LoginForm` and `useLogin`; no internal helpers such as `loginRequest` are re-exported.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does index.ts export only the public contract (LoginForm, useLogin) without leaking internal functions?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Feature files (`api`, `model`, `ui`) import only shared/ or feature-local modules, and the page imports from `@/features/login` only.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does the feature avoid importing from pages/ or widgets/?
